title: epithema dentatum subsp. hispidum
intro: Epithema dentatum subsp. hispidum, also known as the hairy epipactis or hispid Epithema, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Asia and is commonly found in the forests and grasslands of countries such as India, Sri Lanka, and Malaysia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
